$comment: DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Auto-generated from schema/process-schema.json
title: Process Document Schema (v3)
description: The golden source JSON Schema representing a full Process Document instance.

meta.description: System-managed metadata for the overall process.

content.description: Authored content for the overall process.

description.description: The markdown body of the process overview.

roles.description: The teams, functions and individuals involved in the process, and who is responsible for what.

process-steps.description: The ordered steps that make up the process as it runs today, end to end.

exceptions.description: The off-happy-path cases — where the process deviates, stalls or needs manual handling.

pain-points.description: Where the current process hurts — friction, delays and frustrations reported by the people who run it.

metrics.description: The volumes, timings and service levels that quantify how the process performs.

process-gaps.description: Missing or unclear parts of the As-Is picture that need follow-up before it is complete.

country-variations.description: How the process differs by country / jurisdiction — local regulatory or operational variations.

controls.description: The checks and safeguards that keep the process within risk appetite and regulatory limits.

regulation.description: The external rules and supervisory requirements the process must comply with.

control-gaps.description: Weaknesses or holes in the control framework that leave the process exposed.

audit-findings.description: Issues raised by internal or external audit, and their remediation status.

channels.description: The channels through which clients reach the process — branch, online, phone, relationship manager.

touchpoints.description: The individual moments where a client touches the process, step by step.

moments.description: The make-or-break interactions that most shape how clients judge the process.

friction-points.description: Where the client-facing journey is slow, confusing or effortful.

competitor-cx.description: How competitor banks and fintechs run the same client journey.

cx-benchmarks.description: Industry benchmarks and client-expectation research for this kind of journey.

market-trends.description: Market shifts, regulation and technology trends bearing on the process's future.

competitor-innovation.description: What competitors and fintechs are doing that could reshape this process.

innovation-ideas.description: Concrete ideas for improving or reinventing the process.

innovation-risks.description: The risks that come with pursuing the innovation ideas.

to-be-design.description: The redesigned future-state process, step by step.

transformation-decisions.description: The key decisions that shape the move from the current state to the target.

requirements.description: Testable statements of what the target process and systems must do, derived from the transformation decisions and gaps.

dependencies.description: The target process's boundary — the upstream feeders and downstream consumers it connects to.

assumptions.description: What the target design and the transformation rest on — assumptions still to be confirmed.

gap-resolution.description: How each As-Is gap, pain point and control weakness is resolved in the target state.

systems.description: The applications and platforms the process runs on.

integrations.description: The interfaces and data flows that connect those systems.

capabilities.description: Business capabilities the target process needs — the 'what', not the 'how'.

target-applications.description: Applications hosting the target capabilities, with build / buy / configure / keep verdicts.

architecture-decisions.description: ADRs — locked-in technical decisions with their alternatives and consequences.

target-integrations.description: Interfaces between target applications, with patterns (sync / async / event / batch).

components.description: Sub-elements of target applications — services, gateways, workers.

nfrs.description: Non-functional requirements with category, target value and traces back to controls and regulation.

migration-phases.description: Staged sequence from today's systems landscape to the target architecture.

country.description: The country or jurisdiction this variation applies to.

whatDiffers.description: How the process differs in this country versus the baseline. Stylistic constraint: 12–50 words.

whyItDiffers.description: The regulation, market practice or local requirement that drives the difference. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

impact.description: What the variation means for clients, operations or compliance. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

raci.description: RACI assignments mapping process step IDs to responsibility levels (R=Responsible, A=Accountable, C=Consulted, I=Informed).

responsibility.description: What the role owns in the process, stated in one clear sentence. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

inThisProcess.description: The specific steps and controls this role performs in this process. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

owner.description: Who performs this step — a role (e.g. 'Operations Analyst'), or 'system / unassigned' if automated. Never a named individual.

sla.description: The turnaround target for this step (e.g. 'within 2 hours', 'same business day').

condition.description: Any precondition or entry criterion that must hold before this step runs.

transitions.description: Outgoing flow from this step. Field `transitions` — a list of objects, each `{ "to": <target id>, "kind": <normal|branch|loopback|exception>, "when": <condition> }`: the element it flows to (a process-step or exception id), the transition kind, and the condition under which it fires.

description.description: The concrete actions performed in this step, in plain language. Stylistic constraint: 20–95 words.

inputs.description: Each thing the step consumes — documents, data or prior outputs. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

outputs.description: Each thing the step produces and hands to the next step. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

businessValue.description: The risk this step controls or the value it adds. Stylistic constraint: 10–60 words.

category.description: The kind of exception (e.g. data-quality, sanctions hit, missing approval).

frequencyPct.description: Roughly how often the exception occurs, as a percentage of cases.

impactSeverity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — the severity of the exception's effect on the process or client.

handlingOwner.description: The role accountable for resolving this exception.

description.description: What the exception is and the conditions under which it arises. Stylistic constraint: 12–50 words.

handling.description: How the exception is resolved, and by whom. Stylistic constraint: 10–60 words.

impact.description: Effect on cycle time, cost, the client or compliance. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

controlType.description: PREVENTIVE / DETECTIVE / CORRECTIVE — when the control acts relative to the risk.

execution.description: MANUAL / AUTOMATED / HYBRID — whether a person performs the control, the system enforces it, or both (a manual trigger plus an automated execution, e.g. an analyst initiating an automated screening tool).

effectiveness.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — how well the control mitigates its risk, as assessed.

owner.description: The accountable function (e.g. 'Compliance', 'Operations') — never a named individual, even for an automated control.

whatItChecks.description: The specific risk or condition the control verifies. Stylistic constraint: 10–40 words.

controlActivity.description: How the control is performed, by whom and with what. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

riskAddressed.description: The risk that would materialise without this control. Stylistic constraint: 6–40 words.

timing.description: When and how often the control runs. Stylistic constraint: 6–35 words.

domain.description: The regulatory domain (e.g. AML, sanctions, prudential, conduct).

asOf.description: The date this regulatory position was captured or last verified.

source.description: Citation or URL for the rule — the regulation, supervisory guidance or article.

whatItRequires.description: The obligations the regulation imposes on the process. Stylistic constraint: 6–45 words.

whyItApplies.description: Why this regulation is in scope for this process. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

howItIsMet.description: The controls and process steps that satisfy the regulation. Stylistic constraint: 6–40 words.

severity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — the regulatory exposure the gap creates.

gapStatus.description: Where the gap stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

theGap.description: What is missing or non-compliant. Stylistic constraint: 12–45 words.

risk.description: The regulatory or operational exposure the gap creates. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

remediation.description: The actions needed to close the gap. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

auditDate.description: The date the finding was raised by audit.

findingStatus.description: Where the finding stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

severity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — the audit-assessed severity.

finding.description: What the audit observed, with enough context to act on. Stylistic constraint: 15–60 words.

recommendation.description: The auditor's recommended action. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

category.description: The kind of pain point (e.g. manual effort, delay, rework, hand-off friction).

severity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — how badly this hurts the process.

priority.description: P1 / P2 / P3 — how urgently it should be addressed.

description.description: What the pain point is, from the staff or process view. Stylistic constraint: 12–50 words.

impact.description: How it slows, costs or degrades the process. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

rootCause.description: The underlying reason the pain point exists. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

severity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — how badly this friction hurts the client experience.

description.description: Where and how the client experiences friction. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

rootCause.description: Why the friction occurs. Stylistic constraint: 6–40 words.

clientImpact.description: How it affects the client's effort and perception. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

channel.description: The channel this touchpoint occurs on (e.g. branch, app, call centre).

ces.description: Customer Effort Score for this touchpoint, if measured.

whatTheClientDoes.description: The client's action at this touchpoint. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

whatTheBankDoes.description: The bank's response or action at this touchpoint. Stylistic constraint: 6–35 words.

experience.description: How the touchpoint feels — effort, clarity and emotion. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

sentiment.description: positive / negative / critical — the client's emotional read of this moment.

theMoment.description: The specific moment in the client journey. Stylistic constraint: 6–35 words.

whyItMatters.description: Why this moment shapes the client's overall perception. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

designImplication.description: What the process should do about this moment. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

channelType.description: DIGITAL or ASSISTED — self-service, or mediated by staff.

description.description: What the channel is and how clients reach it. Stylistic constraint: 5–35 words.

usage.description: When and how much the channel is used in onboarding. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

competitor.description: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

asOf.description: The date this competitor CX observation was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the observation.

theJourney.description: How a European corporate bank runs this client journey. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: What it means for this process — the experience gap it opens. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The review, case study or announcement that evidences it. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

competitor.description: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

asOf.description: The date this competitor CX observation was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the observation.

theJourney.description: How a global corporate bank runs this client journey. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: What it means for this process — the experience gap it opens. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The review, case study or announcement that evidences it. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

competitor.description: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

asOf.description: The date this competitor CX observation was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the observation.

theJourney.description: How a fintech runs this client journey. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: What it means for this process — the experience gap it opens. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The review, case study or announcement that evidences it. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

asOf.description: The date this benchmark figure was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the benchmark.

theBenchmark.description: The industry CX standard or client expectation, described plainly. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: How this process measures against it. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The specific figure or signal — survey, study or standard. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

target.description: The goal / SLA figure (e.g. 'same business day'). The figure stated in a process-design document is almost always the target, not the measured actual.

value.description: The measured actual today. Leave empty if only a target is known — an unmeasured metric is itself worth flagging.

trend.description: The direction of travel — improving, flat or worsening — if known.

definition.description: What the metric measures and how it is calculated. Stylistic constraint: 6–35 words.

currentReading.description: The latest value and the trend. Stylistic constraint: 4–35 words.

whyItMatters.description: What the metric tells the process owner. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

area.description: The area the gap sits in (e.g. As-Is process, controls, systems).

gapStatus.description: Where the gap stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

theGap.description: What is missing in how the process is run or measured. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

impact.description: What the gap prevents or puts at risk. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

nextStep.description: The recommended action to close the gap. Stylistic constraint: 8–35 words.

horizon.description: regulatory-deadline / near-term / emerging — how soon the trend bites.

asOf.description: The date this trend was captured or last verified.

source.description: Citation or URL — the market study, analyst report or article.

theTrend.description: The market or industry trend, described plainly. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: Why the trend matters for this process. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The specific figure, statistic or signal that evidences the trend — the data point, not a re-listing of the source. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

competitor.description: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

asOf.description: The date this competitor observation was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the observation.

theMove.description: The innovation a European corporate bank is pursuing, described plainly. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: What it means for this process — the competitive gap it opens. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The announcement, report or launch that evidences the move. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

competitor.description: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

asOf.description: The date this competitor observation was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the observation.

theMove.description: The innovation a global corporate bank is pursuing, described plainly. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: What it means for this process — the competitive gap it opens. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The announcement, report or launch that evidences the move. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

competitor.description: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

asOf.description: The date this competitor observation was captured.

source.description: Citation or URL for the observation.

theMove.description: The innovation a fintech is pursuing, described plainly. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

relevance.description: What it means for this process — the competitive gap it opens. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

evidence.description: The announcement, report or launch that evidences the move. Stylistic constraint: 8–40 words.

category.description: The kind of idea (e.g. automation, new channel, control redesign).

strategicFit.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — how well the idea aligns with strategy.

complexity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — the effort and difficulty of delivering it.

theIdea.description: The proposed change, described plainly. Stylistic constraint: 12–55 words.

expectedBenefit.description: The outcome it would deliver and for whom. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

feasibility.description: Effort, dependencies and the main risks. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

severity.description: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — the severity of this risk if it materialises.

theRisk.description: What could go wrong with the innovation. Stylistic constraint: 12–50 words.

likelihoodImpact.description: How likely the risk is and how severe its effect. Stylistic constraint: 10–45 words.

mitigation.description: How the risk can be reduced or managed. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

targetDescription.description: How the step or process works in the target state. Stylistic constraint: 20–95 words.

whatChanges.description: Each concrete change from the As-Is. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

rationale.description: Why the target state is designed this way. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

decisionType.description: The kind of decision (e.g. build/buy, scope, sequencing).

decisionStatus.description: Where the decision stands — proposed, agreed, or deferred.

theDecision.description: What was decided. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

optionsConsidered.description: The alternatives that were weighed. Stylistic constraint: 2–5 items.

rationale.description: Why this option was chosen over the others. Stylistic constraint: 12–60 words.

validationArea.description: The area this gap was surfaced in during validation.

gapStatus.description: Where the gap stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

theGap.description: The As-Is gap being resolved. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

resolution.description: How the target state closes the gap. Stylistic constraint: 12–60 words.

status.description: Where the resolution currently stands. Stylistic constraint: 5–30 words.

systemType.description: CORE / SUPPORTING / EXTERNAL — the system's role in this process.

purpose.description: What the system is for. Stylistic constraint: 4–30 words.

roleInThisProcess.description: Which steps use the system and how. Stylistic constraint: 6–40 words.

whatConnects.description: The two systems the integration links. Stylistic constraint: 6–30 words.

whatFlows.description: Each kind of data that moves across the integration. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

reqType.description: FUNCTIONAL or NON-FUNCTIONAL — what the requirement constrains.

moscow.description: MUST / SHOULD / COULD / WONT — MoSCoW priority for this requirement.

requirement.description: One testable statement of what the target process or system must do. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

rationale.description: The transformation decision or gap this requirement comes from. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

acceptanceCriteria.description: How you would confirm the requirement is met. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

direction.description: UPSTREAM / DOWNSTREAM / BIDIRECTIONAL — how this process and the dependency relate.

theDependency.description: The connected process or party at the boundary. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

whatCrossesTheBoundary.description: The data, trigger or artefact handed over, and in which direction. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

whyItMatters.description: The impact if the dependency fails or runs late. Stylistic constraint: 6–40 words.

assumptionStatus.description: OPEN / CONFIRMED / INVALIDATED — whether the assumption still holds.

theAssumption.description: What is being assumed. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

whyItIsUnconfirmed.description: What is unknown, and why it could not be verified. Stylistic constraint: 8–45 words.

impactIfWrong.description: What breaks if the assumption does not hold. Stylistic constraint: 6–40 words.

criticality.description: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — how essential this capability is to the target process.

reuse.description: NEW / REUSED — whether this is a fresh capability or one already in the catalog.

owningDomain.description: The business domain that owns this capability (e.g. 'Case management', 'Payments').

description.description: What the capability does — the function from the consumer's perspective. Stylistic constraint: 20–80 words.

inputsAndOutputs.description: What the capability needs to operate, and what it produces. Stylistic constraint: 15–60 words.

boundaries.description: What this capability is NOT responsible for — adjacent capabilities it relies on. Stylistic constraint: 10–40 words.

verdict.description: BUILD / BUY / CONFIGURE / KEEP — the disposition of this app in the target state.

vendor.description: Vendor name for BUY apps; tech stack for BUILD; vendor + 'configured' for CONFIGURE.

owningDomain.description: The business domain that owns this app.

costBand.description: Build / run / annual licence cost band (rough budgeting estimate).

rationale.description: Why this app, and why this verdict — what the alternatives were. Stylistic constraint: 20–80 words.

techStack.description: Concrete technologies, hosting region, deployment model. Stylistic constraint: 10–60 words.

risks.description: Risks introduced or carried by this disposition (vendor lock-in, residency, capacity, etc.). Stylistic constraint: 1–5 items.

adrStatus.description: DRAFT / PROPOSED / ACCEPTED / REJECTED / SUPERSEDED — the ADR lifecycle state.

owner.description: The accountable architect (a role, not a named person).

domain.description: The architectural domain this decision belongs to (e.g. 'Case management', 'Payments').

decision.description: What we decided to do. Stylistic constraint: 15–80 words.

context.description: Why the decision is needed — the situation, constraints and forces in play. Stylistic constraint: 30–120 words.

alternativesConsidered.description: The options we weighed and why each was not chosen. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

consequences.description: What follows from the decision — new dependencies, NFRs, risks, technical debt. Stylistic constraint: 2–8 items.

pattern.description: SYNC / ASYNC / EVENT / BATCH — the interaction style.

direction.description: ONE-WAY / TWO-WAY / PUB-SUB — the data-flow direction.

contract.description: Interface contract reference — OpenAPI 3.1, AsyncAPI 2.6, CMIS, Snowflake stage, etc.

volume.description: Approximate volume + latency target (e.g. '~12k req/day · p95 380ms').

purpose.description: Why the two apps need to talk, and what data flows. Stylistic constraint: 15–60 words.

contractDetails.description: Auth, schema, idempotency, versioning — the contract specifics. Stylistic constraint: 15–80 words.

failureMode.description: Behaviour when the downstream is unavailable or returns an error. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

dataStore.description: The persistence layer this component owns, if any (e.g. 'Postgres 16').

responsibility.description: What this component owns within its application. Stylistic constraint: 15–60 words.

technicalDetail.description: Tech choices, data ownership, scaling profile. Stylistic constraint: 15–80 words.

category.description: PERFORMANCE / AVAILABILITY / SECURITY / COMPLIANCE / SCALABILITY — the NFR family.

target.description: The concrete, testable target (e.g. 'p95 < 1.2s', 'RTO ≤ 4h', '10 years immutable').

owner.description: The team accountable for meeting the NFR (a role, not a person).

definition.description: What is being measured and at what threshold. Stylistic constraint: 15–60 words.

measurement.description: How the target is observed in production — instrumentation and method. Stylistic constraint: 10–50 words.

verification.description: How the NFR is tested before go-live — load test, audit, etc. Stylistic constraint: 10–60 words.

phaseStatus.description: PLANNED / IN-FLIGHT / DONE — current execution state.

startQuarter.description: Calendar quarter the phase begins (e.g. '2026 Q3').

scope.description: What this phase delivers and what it explicitly does not. Stylistic constraint: 20–100 words.

acceptanceCriteria.description: What must be true for the phase to be called done. Stylistic constraint: 2–6 items.

risks.description: Risks that could push the phase late or break dependent phases. Stylistic constraint: 1–5 items.
